concept: Modular medium-voltage DC (1-35kV) hybrid solid-state circuit breaker productized for datacenter campuses, microgrids, and MVDC collection
painful_job: DC fault interruption is the documented showstopper for MVDC: no converged breaker topology, no product category, forcing point-to-point architectures and over-engineered protection while datacenter DC distribution scales.
product: A standardized, modular hybrid SSCB (mechanical ultra-fast disconnect + SiC interruption branch + energy absorber) in 1-35kV/1-4kA frames with coordinated non-unit protection relaying, sold as engineered-but-catalogued units rather than per-project HVDC engineering.
frontier_vision: Enable meshed MVDC campuses and DC industrial parks; expand to MVDC collection for storage/generation and eventually HVDC tap protection.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: comparative topology study and scaled (kV-class) interruption prototypes in a university high-power lab; track ARPA-E DC-GRIDS cohort results (L02-034). 2027: SiC interruption-duty die-characterization study (di/dt, avalanche energy, series sharing vs press-pack IGBT baselines) and fail-short press-pack qualification concept - device-strategy workstream imported from wave-F seed F-08 (2026-07-13); cheap, publishable, and feeds the module design regardless of breaker-market timing (L08-001/003/004/017). 2028: 10kV single-module interruption demo at a national-lab test facility (ORNL SSCB lineage, L08-051); IP on module scaling and relay coordination. 2029: co-develop requirements with one hyperscaler campus power team and one DC-GRIDS performer; secure test-bed MOU.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: MVDC datacenter/industrial distribution becomes economic ahead of protection availability; 2030 is when first US MVDC campus pilots need catalogued breakers, before ABB/Hitachi commit to a productized mid-voltage line.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US MVDC campus distribution pilots and DC-GRIDS-seeded utility demonstrations reaching procurement 2030-2033 (ARPA-E DC-GRIDS 2026 awards, L02-034; datacenter electrification order growth, L08-033; Southern Spirit-class HVDC construction from 2029, L08-041).
competition_outlook_2030: Hitachi Energy hybrid breaker heritage (L08-052), Siemens/GE per-project offerings, 1-2 ARPA-E-spun startups; academic topologies still non-converged (L08-001/003/004), so productization discipline is the differentiator.
timing_window_risk: MVDC adoption could stay point-to-point (breaker-avoiding) longer than expected; per-project incumbents may bundle protection into converter stations.
extreme_edge: Millisecond-to-microsecond staged interruption with no natural current zero at tens of kV - the hardest unsolved switching problem in power delivery.
first_experiment: 2028: 10kV/2kA hybrid module interrupting a simulated pole-to-pole fault in <2 ms with >=100 operations without refurbishment, at a national-lab or university high-power facility.
founder_fit_note: Founder circuits, controls, and embedded-systems skills transfer to the breaker's sensing, relaying, and trigger electronics, but medium-voltage kA-class power hardware, the mechanical ultra-fast disconnect, and certification programs lie outside the profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13 R2: imported P3R2-F-08's (wave-F REJECT) device-strategy workstream - 2027 SiC interruption-duty die-characterization study (di/dt, avalanche energy, series sharing vs press-pack IGBT baselines) and fail-short press-pack qualification concept; cheap, publishable, useful regardless of breaker-market timing. No new source IDs: L08-001/003/004/017 already cited and eligible; F-08's L08-016 is not accepted in the ledger and was NOT imported.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical MVDC hybrid breaker. Documented showstopper niche; MVDC adoption pace and $1.5M pre-company experiment are the honest risks; lean on DC-GRIDS/ORNL facilities.

concept: Containerized MW-class grid-emulation test system for NERC PRC-029-1 / ride-through verification of inverter-based resources at plant sites
named_buyer_examples: US battery-storage and solar owner-operators subject to PRC-029-1
GE Vernova and other inverter OEMs
painful_job: NERC PRC-029-1 (FERC Order 909, July 2025) imposes ride-through requirements on the US IBR fleet; owners and OEMs lack field-deployable MW-scale hardware to verify plant-level ride-through, and grid-forming claims are contested in procurement (NESO awarded zero GFM contracts).
product: A trailerized 1-5 MW back-to-back converter grid emulator (voltage/frequency/phase-jump event playback, weak-grid emulation) with automated PRC-029/IEEE-2800-style test sequencing and evidence-grade reporting.
frontier_vision: The standard verification instrument for a re-inverterized grid: ride-through, grid-forming capability attestation, and interconnection model validation as a hardware-anchored service network.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: track PRC-029 compliance guidance and audit findings; design event-playback converter control in an academic/PHIL lab; interview 20+ owner-operators and 5 OEM compliance teams. 2028: 250kW prototype emulator validated against lab PHIL benchmarks. 2029: MW-unit design freeze, LOIs from two owner-operators facing re-verification. FIX gate (2027): obtain written regional-entity compliance guidance or two owner-operator LOIs evidencing that PRC-029 verification will require/pay for field MW hardware rather than EMT-model submissions; if model-based compliance suffices, kill the standalone thesis and fold the test-sequencing/reporting stack into P3R2-E-14's qualification platform.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Enforcement and event-triggered re-verification peak in the early 2030s as the standard beds in and the IBR fleet grows; incumbent test-equipment firms sell lab simulators, not field MW hardware.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: NERC PRC-029-1 compliance/enforcement phase-in and post-disturbance re-verification wave across the US IBR fleet, 2030-2034 (L08-043). CONDITION (unproven): the standard may be satisfiable with EMT-model evidence alone; field MW-hardware demand must be affirmatively documented (regional-entity guidance or owner LOIs) by 2027 or the seed fails. GFM-attestation leg dropped: no US operator GFM procurement spec exists in the atlas and the closest procurement evidence is negative (NESO awarded zero GFM contracts); AEMO precedent (L08-048) retained as watch-item only, not as demand.
competition_outlook_2030: NREL/Sandia lab facilities (non-commercial), PHIL vendors (OPAL-RT/Typhoon class) at lab scale, engineering consultancies with models only; syncon vendors (L08-036/037) as substitute stability providers rather than verifiers. Positioning per adjudication: field-hardware complement to P3R2-E-14's relay/HIL qualification platform, not a competitor to it.
timing_window_risk: If NERC accepts model-based (EMT simulation) compliance evidence broadly, field hardware demand shrinks to OEM labs; regulatory softening is possible.
extreme_edge: Playing back real grid disturbance records at MW power into a live plant - hardware-in-the-loop at fleet scale.
first_experiment: 2028: 250kW grid-emulator prototype reproducing a recorded multi-phase-jump disturbance within 2% envelope error against a PHIL reference, with automated PRC-029-format reporting.
founder_fit_note: Automated test sequencing, data acquisition, and controls experience transfer to the event-playback and evidence-grade reporting layer, while MW-class back-to-back converter design and trailerized power integration are outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: demand mechanism hedged (field-MW-hardware requirement unproven vs EMT-model compliance; 2027 documentation gate added); GFM-attestation leg dropped on the NESO negative finding (AEMO kept as watch-item only); repositioned as the field-hardware complement to E-14 with a fold-in path if the gate fails.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Real instrument gap; demand mechanism unproven if model-based compliance suffices.

concept: US merchant NEG-coating and low-outgassing surface engineering line for UHV/XHV chambers (accelerators, quantum, EUV, space simulation)
painful_job: NEG coating - the pumping architecture accelerators specify - is effectively single-sourced globally (SAES), and hydrogen-outgassing control still requires 30-hour 250C bakeouts baked into every chamber schedule; US labs and OEMs carry single-point-of-supply and schedule risk.
product: A domestic merchant coating line: NEG (TiZrV-class) sputter coating of beam pipes and chambers, vacuum-firing/low-H treatment, and certified outgassing metrology, sold per-part with quality data - a second source with US supply-chain preference.
frontier_vision: Own engineered vacuum surfaces: from accelerator beam pipes to XHV quantum and EUV chambers, then integrated getter-pump products.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: replicate published NEG-coating recipes on a university sputter rig; validate pumping speed/activation temperature against literature; survey US lab procurement specs. 2028: coat and qualify pilot beam-pipe sections for one DOE lab under a cooperative agreement; certify outgassing test bench to lab standards. 2029: production-line design, LOIs from two labs and one chamber OEM.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: US accelerator upgrade projects and quantum/EUV chamber demand rise into the 2030s while the sole incumbent remains foreign and capacity-limited; domestic-sourcing preferences strengthen for DOE-funded procurement.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: DOE accelerator-upgrade and QIS-facility construction procurements 2030-2034 (DOE $625M QIS centers, L13-028) plus continuing ITER-class vacuum tenders (L07-041/042/043/044) and NASA facility sustainment (L07-035).
competition_outlook_2030: SAES Getters (sole merchant incumbent, L07-037), in-house coating at CERN-affiliated labs, general vacuum houses without NEG physics; ULVAC-class majors could enter if the niche grows.
timing_window_risk: Niche size may stay small if accelerator budgets stall; SAES could add US capacity and close the sovereignty gap.
extreme_edge: Reducing hydrogen outgassing by >10,000x and replacing lumped pumps with the chamber wall itself as the pump.
first_experiment: 2027: deposit TiZrV NEG on 1-m test pipes, demonstrate activation at <=200C and sticking coefficients within 20% of published SAES-class performance, verified by an independent lab outgassing bench.
founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: certified outgassing metrology draws on instrumentation and data-acquisition skills, but the core sputter-coating, vacuum-firing, and surface-chemistry process line is outside the founder's electrical-engineering profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed B-12 (CN NEG line). No content imported: the license-into-China model was rejected by the adjudicator as IP leakage in a process-know-how business with CEPC-dependent lumpy demand; A-05 remains US-primary with no CN chapter.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Single-source (SAES) supply gap + US sovereignty preference; process know-how moat; modest vision ceiling accepted; SAES adding US capacity is the kill risk.

concept: Vendor-neutral ion-energy-distribution metrology + tailored-waveform bias control retrofit for plasma etch chambers
named_buyer_examples: US leading-edge fabs (Intel/Micron archetype, CHIPS-funded expansions)
AMEC (CN, via partitioned >=28nm entity, from B-05)
NAURA / Piotech (CN, via partitioned >=28nm entity, from B-05)
painful_job: Tailored-voltage-waveform biasing for atomic-scale etch is an unsettled 2024-26 research frontier; fabs and OEMs lack independent ion-energy-distribution measurement and closed-loop waveform control - they tune blind against drifting sheaths at sub-2nm nodes.
product: A chamber-mounted IEDF sensor (RF-immune retarding analyzer / derived-signal estimator) plus a waveform-synthesis bias controller that closes the loop on ion energy rather than on electrical setpoints; retrofits onto existing generators. Merged extensions: (1) company structure = two-entity export partition (from C-06) - the US entity serves leading-edge OCP/fab customers, a separate CN-serving line is limited to mature-node (>=28nm) applications; (2) CN product chapter (from B-05): waveform-bias RF delivery for AMEC/NAURA/Piotech-class OEM localization programs, mature-node scope only; (3) arc/ESC-discharge event-detection and forensics layer (from B-21), contingent on FTO analysis - patent-dense field.
frontier_vision: Ion-energy-defined processing as the control standard across etch, deposition, and emerging plasma applications - the measurement layer incumbent generator vendors do not own.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: build IEDF estimation methods on a university ICP/CCP reactor; benchmark against gridded analyzers; publish and patent sensor+control coupling. 2028: alpha retrofit on an OEM lab chamber under JDA; quantify etch-profile improvement on test wafers. 2029: SEMI-standards engagement and pilot with one US fab process-development group. Merged gates: export-control counsel review of the two-entity partition is a 2027 gate (before any CN-facing work); FTO analysis precedes any investment in the B-21 event-detection layer. KR validation channel (from E-03): Samsung TVW co-authorship pull (L06-009) and the Korean plasma-diagnostics base (L06-024/025) as reference-user and validation channel.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Waveform-capable generators (AE Everest class) proliferate 2026-29, creating the installed base that then needs independent metrology and control by 2030; generator vendors sell sources, not vendor-neutral measurement.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: sub-2nm/backside-power etch capacity ramps at CHIPS-funded US fabs 2030-2033 and second-generation waveform-bias platform adoption (Advanced Energy new-platform ramp evidence, L06-039). CN (independent leg, merged from B-05/C-06; mature-node scope): AMEC/NAURA/Piotech supplier-localization programs under the 15th FYP - AMEC etch revenue RMB7.28bn +54.7% (L06-048), the Hengyunchang/Injet domestic-substitution wave (L06-042/043/044), and the <12% RF/precision-component localization gap (L06-054) those OEMs are documented to be closing; served only through the partitioned >=28nm entity, subject to the 2027 export-counsel gate.
competition_outlook_2030: Advanced Energy/MKS/Comet embedding partial capability in generators (L06-039, L06-049); Impedans-class plasma diagnostics; tool-OEM internal sensors. Neutral closed-loop IEDF control remains open.
timing_window_risk: OEMs may lock chamber data behind proprietary interfaces; if generator vendors integrate good-enough IEDF inference by 2030 the retrofit market narrows to process-development labs.
extreme_edge: Controlling ion energy distributions to electron-volt precision through an RF sheath at 100+ MHz superposition - measurement where physics fights the measurement.
first_experiment: 2027-2028: closed-loop IEDF control demo on a lab reactor holding a programmed bimodal ion-energy peak within +/-2 eV across intentional pressure/chemistry drift; correlate to etch-profile metrics.
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: the RF-immune ion-energy sensor, signal-derived estimation, and closed-loop waveform bias controller map directly onto founder sensors/instrumentation, circuits, controls, and electromagnetics skills.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed C-06 (US/CN two-entity export partition as company structure), B-05 (CN mature-node RF-delivery chapter with localization evidence), E-03 (Samsung co-authorship pull + KR validation channel), B-21 (event-detection/forensics layer, FTO-gated). primary_market US->US+CN and china_beachhead->true because the CN demand leg is independent and cited to eligible CN evidence (L06-042/043/044/048/054); honesty conditions documented - mature-node (>=28nm) scope only, 2027 export-control counsel gate, leading-edge line stays in the US entity.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical TVW/IEDF control. Merge in C-06 dual-entity export partition, E-03 KR validation channel, B-21 event-detection layer. Export-control counsel is a 2027 gate.

concept: Sub-100ms no-overshoot gas delivery: pressure-insensitive fast MFC with inline transient metrology for ALD/ALE throughput
named_buyer_examples: etch/deposition tool OEMs (Lam/Applied archetype via gas-panel integrators)
painful_job: SEMI E17 codifies transient response as the constraint; thermal MFCs still overshoot on fast steps, and ALD/ALE cycle counts keep rising - gas switching time is now direct throughput money on $100M+ tools fleets.
product: A fast-actuation MFC (pressure-based sensing + model-predictive valve control) hitting sub-100 ms settled steps without overshoot, plus an inline transient-verification module that gives OEMs SEMI E17-style evidence per channel.
frontier_vision: The gas-delivery timing layer for atomic-layer processing everywhere - semiconductors first, then precision CVD industries.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: control-theory and sensor work on a lab flow bench (building on estimated-output-feedback literature); prototype actuator/sensing stack. 2028: third-party SEMI E17-protocol characterization vs incumbent MFCs; JDA discussions with one gas-panel integrator. 2029: alpha units on an OEM development tool; reliability data. FIX additions: 2026-27 includes a quantified $/tool/year throughput-value model vs OEM qualification switching costs, and broadening of the technical base beyond L06-026/L06-034 (additional peer-reviewed transient-flow sources to be added in P4). Hard 2028 gate: a paid evaluation or LOI from one gas-panel integrator/OEM evidencing dual-sourcing appetite on transient specs; absent it, shelve the seed.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: ALD/ALE step counts and gas-panel channel counts keep climbing through 2030; incumbents optimize steady-state accuracy and are slow to re-architect sensing; a demonstrable throughput delta converts to design-ins on next tool generations 2030-2033.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Next-generation US fab tool builds 2030-2033 (CHIPS-funded capacity, Edwards subfab precedent L06-033) and an expected SEMI E17 revision cycle tightening transient specs (standard vintage evidence, L06-035).
competition_outlook_2030: Horiba/Brooks/MKS/Fujikin incumbent MFC lines (market structure via L07-049 gas-panel concentration); pressure-based fast MFCs may partially close the gap - differentiation must be verified transient evidence.
timing_window_risk: Incumbent catch-up is the main risk; OEM qualification cycles are long and single-sourced relationships sticky. Adjudication: incumbent (Horiba/Brooks/MKS) catch-up could close the window before 2030; the 2028 dual-sourcing gate is the go/no-go.
extreme_edge: Metering reactive gases to 1% in tens of milliseconds against varying upstream pressure - flow control at chemical-reaction timescales.
first_experiment: 2027: bench demonstration of 0-90% flow steps settling <100 ms with zero overshoot across 2x upstream-pressure variation, measured by an independent rate-of-rise standard.
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: pressure-based sensing, model-predictive valve control, and inline transient metrology sit squarely in founder instrumentation, controls, and data-acquisition skills, with only the fluidic/valve mechanics outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 paid-eval/LOI dual-sourcing gate added; $/tool/year throughput-value-vs-switching-cost quantification added; P4 task to broaden the technical base beyond L06-026/034.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Incumbent (Horiba/Brooks/MKS) catch-up could close window pre-2030.

concept: Modular rad-tolerant GaN power processing units (2-20kW class) with standardized interfaces for electric propulsion and spacecraft power
named_buyer_examples: SDA constellation primes (Lockheed, Northrop, Rocket Lab)
painful_job: PPU topology never converged after 50 years; the flagship US supplier now sits inside a missile-focused conglomerate; every program pays fresh NRE, and small/mid satellites are priced out of EP by PPU cost and mass - while GaN/SiC space qualification remains unfinished.
product: A qualified modular PPU family: rad-tolerant GaN power stages, standardized anode/keeper/mag supplies, common telemetry and (AIAA S-122 / ECSS-E-ST-20-20C-aligned) interfaces, scaling 2-20kW by module count with a documented radiation test dossier. Merged extensions: SEE-map-first sequencing - publish device SEE/TID maps before module qualification - and a 'qualified-once, reused-everywhere' qualification file reused across thruster classes (from D-15); ECSS-E-ST-20-20C / AIAA S-122 qualification framing hardened as the interface between merchant modules and primes (from E-10).
frontier_vision: The merchant power-electronics layer for the electric spacecraft: EP drive, high-voltage buses, and eventually nuclear-surface-power conversion electronics.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: publish GaN converter SEE/TID characterization (heavy-ion campaigns via university facilities, building on L09-103/104 methods); breadboard 5kW discharge supply. 2028: SBIR-track qualification unit; thermal-vac + radiation test of the standard module; interface spec whitepaper circulated to primes. 2029: EM unit integrated with a partner thruster (Busek-class) in a vacuum-facility hot-fire test.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Proliferated constellations and NASA sustainment demand rise into the 2030s while Aerojet/L3Harris prioritizes missiles; a modern GaN modular line at half the mass/cost has no US merchant incumbent.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: NASA Gateway/Mars-logistics EP sustainment and SDA Tranche 4/5-class proliferated procurement 2030-2034 (AEPS $67M and NEXT-C $18.4M precedents L09-034/L09-012; PPE supply chain L09-043; SDA T3 $3.5B scale signal L09-039 - EP content flagged unconfirmed). Additional trigger (from D-15): Space Force Maneuverable-GEO-class programs (L09-037). SDA EP content remains flagged unconfirmed - P4 must close it.
competition_outlook_2030: L3Harris/Aerojet (L09-041), Busek/Moog in-house, EPC Space at component level (L09-054/055), JAXA/Furukawa line in Japan (L09-042); Rocket Lab vertical integration (L09-114).
timing_window_risk: Primes may vertically integrate PPUs; GaN SEE derating could erode the mass advantage; NASA budget volatility.
extreme_edge: kW-class GaN switching that survives heavy-ion strikes at full bus voltage - power density where derating rules were written for silicon.
first_experiment: 2027-2028: 5kW GaN discharge-supply breadboard passing heavy-ion testing at full operating voltage (no destructive SEE at mission-representative LET) plus efficiency >96%; results published as the qualification baseline.
secondary_markets: JP: JAXA-Furukawa J-SPARC GaN Hall-thruster power-supply commercialization as validation-pattern partner/licensee (L09-042) [from E-10]
KR: KARI full-electric-propulsion GEO design studies as an emergent 2030s buyer (L09-019) [from E-10]
founder_fit_note: Power-stage circuit design, standardized telemetry, and control interfaces draw on founder circuits and embedded-systems skills, but radiation-effects qualification and space-flight hardware processes are outside the profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed E-10 (ECSS/AIAA qualification framing, JAXA-Furukawa J-SPARC partner pattern, KARI emergent-buyer note) and D-15 (SEE-map-first sequencing, qualified-once qualification-file framing, Space Force Maneuverable-GEO trigger L09-037). Prime vertical integration remains the standing risk.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical GaN PPU. SDA EP content unconfirmed - P4 must close. Merge in E-10 ECSS/AIAA framing + JAXA-Furukawa pattern; D-15 SEE-map-first sequencing. Prime vertical integration is standing risk.

concept: 300C-class mixed-signal instrumentation electronics platform (SiC/SOI) for superhot geothermal, downhole, and turbine environments
painful_job: Commercial high-temp ICs top out at 225C and the two incumbent lines are obsolete/exiting (CISSOID CHT last-time-buy; Honeywell legacy), while funded US programs now target 375C+ reservoirs and 800C ICs - existing buyers face a live sourcing gap between what they can buy and where they must operate.
product: A qualified 300C mixed-signal instrumentation platform: SiC/SOI front-ends (ADC, mux, drivers), high-temp passives/packaging stack, and a reference logging/telemetry module - sold as chipset + qualified module to downhole/geothermal tool OEMs. Merged extensions: packaging-first qualification strategy - advanced die-attach/ceramic packaging is the documented true bottleneck (L15-005, L15-025) and leads the roadmap (from E-09); a 400C SiC-JFET instrumentation tier (NASA-heritage JFET front ends, partitioned hot-front-end + insulated-DSP architecture) as the roadmap step above 300C (from D-14).
frontier_vision: Native electronics for hot places: superhot geothermal, aero-engine and hypersonics instrumentation, Venus-class space missions, and close-coupled power-device gate drive.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: 300C building-block circuits via university SiC/SOI runs (Arkansas/NASA-Glenn lineage methods); packaging/die-attach thermal-cycling study. 2028: 1,000-hour 300C soak of a mixed-signal front-end; present at FORGE/SUPERHOT community events; discovery with 10+ tool OEM engineering leads. 2029: field-representative logging-module prototype survives 500 hours in a heated test well/autoclave; LOI from one service company. Added roadmap steps: the 1,000h powered-soak artifact is the flagship qualification datum (from E-09); a FORGE-well field-trial step for the logging module follows in 2029 (from D-14). B-17's China chapter NOT imported: the atlas contains no named Chinese HT-electronics procurement (self-flagged inferential), so CN stays out of the demand base; revisit only if named evidence appears.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: SUPERHOT/FORGE-funded drilling reaches wells hotter than any purchasable electronics by ~2030; CISSOID/Honeywell exit leaves buyers without a roadmap exactly as EGS scales on datacenter power demand.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Superhot-rock EGS drilling campaigns 2030-2034 seeded by ARPA-E SUPERHOT (awards from Nov 2025, L15-029) and DOE FORGE cohorts (L15-027/028), plus Baker Hughes-scale geothermal orders (L15-040) and SLB-Ormat commercial EGS expansion (L15-041); DARPA THERMAL Phase II completion ~2029 derisks 500C+ follow-on (L15-026).
competition_outlook_2030: TI HT-line silicon (175-210C), CISSOID remnants (EU), in-house electronics at SLB/Halliburton (200C class), Ozark IC-class niche design houses; nobody selling a qualified 300C platform.
timing_window_risk: If superhot drilling slips, the 300C market stays program-funded; oilfield in-house teams may capture the niche for their own tools only.
extreme_edge: Mixed-signal circuits living for months at 300C where every commercial IC datasheet ends - the temperature frontier of electronics as a product.
first_experiment: 2028: 300C/1,000-hour continuous operation of an 8-channel SiC/SOI acquisition front-end (ADC + mux + reference) with <1% drift, independently witnessed; thermal-cycling of the packaging stack to 500 cycles.
secondary_markets: JP: Fukushima-decommissioning robotics ecosystem as a qualified-electronics side market (L15-011) [from E-09]
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer on the mixed-signal instrumentation front-ends, data-acquisition chain, and reference logging module, which are core founder skills; the high-temperature packaging and die-attach materials work that gates the product is outside the profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed E-09 (packaging-first strategy, 1,000h powered-soak artifact, JP decommissioning side market), D-14 (400C SiC-JFET tier, FORGE field-trial step), B-17 (rejected CN chapter - kept out of demand base for lack of named CN procurement).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical 300C platform. Best demand-timing story in family (SUPERHOT/FORGE vs CISSOID/Honeywell exit). Merge in E-09 packaging-first strategy + JP side market; D-14 400C tier.

concept: Chiplet-era thermal interface: manufacturable liquid-metal/sintered TIM system with per-package reliability engineering for 1,000W+ AI packages
painful_job: 2.5D/3D chiplet packages create hundreds-of-watts hotspots that existing TIM1/TIM1.5 materials cannot serve - the tradeoff among bulk conductivity, bond-line resistance, and thermo-mechanical reliability is a documented, unresolved first-order constraint on AI package design.
product: A TIM system, not a paste and not a service: a productized SKU family (liquid-metal/sintered-metal composites with engineered barriers/wetting layers, in defined viscosity/bond-line grades) plus a licensed dispensing+containment process package (equipment recipe, containment design rules, QA spec) that packagers run on their own lines; accelerated-reliability data (power cycling, pump-out, corrosion) is the sales artifact. Per-package engineering is limited to paid qualification programs that feed the standard SKU grades - not open-ended engineering services.
frontier_vision: Own the die-to-cold-plate interface across AI, power electronics, and RF - the last 50 microns that set junction temperature.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university materials program on barrier metallurgy and pump-out physics; build power-cycling reliability rig. 2028: demonstrate <5 mm2K/W effective resistance with 1,000-cycle stability on test vehicles; engage one packager's advanced-development group. 2029: pilot line process definition with a dispensing-equipment partner; qualification plan agreed with an anchor customer. FIX gate (2028): anchor-packager LOI on the SKU + process-license model. Kill gate: the head-to-head reliability delta vs two named incumbent metal TIMs on identical test vehicles - no statistically significant advantage means kill.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: 3D-stacked accelerators push local flux toward 1,000 W/cm2 by the early 2030s; incumbent silicone/graphite TIM suppliers optimize for volume manufacturability, leaving the extreme-flux, engineered-per-package segment unserved.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US advanced-packaging capacity ramps and 4-5kW accelerator packages 2030-2033 (device-level flux trajectory L14-010; NVIDIA rack-scale liquid transition L14-044; COOLERCHIPS-funded co-design ecosystem L14-030).
competition_outlook_2030: Honeywell/Shin-Etsu/Dow incumbent TIMs, Indium Corp metal TIMs, in-house packager solutions; academic liquid-metal work commoditizing basic formulations - differentiation is reliability engineering + application process.
timing_window_risk: Packagers may design around TIM1 via direct-liquid or embedded cooling; incumbent materials giants can outspend if the segment proves large.
extreme_edge: Metallic interfaces that survive thousands of power cycles without pump-out or intermetallic failure at fluxes where polymers burn out.
first_experiment: 2028: test-vehicle campaign showing <=5 mm2K/W with <10% degradation over 1,000 power cycles at 500 W/cm2 local flux, benchmarked against two incumbent TIMs on identical vehicles.
founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: this is a materials/metallurgy and process-licensing play, with founder skills contributing only peripherally via instrumentation for power-cycling and accelerated-reliability test rigs.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: converted consulting-shaped offering into a productized SKU + dispensing-process license model; 2028 anchor-packager LOI added; the two-incumbent head-to-head reliability delta made the explicit kill gate.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Consulting-shaped as written; incumbent materials giants can outspend once segment proves.

concept: Ruggedized multi-megawatt charging power systems (MCS/IEC TS 63379-aligned) for US ports, rail yards, and surface mines
named_buyer_examples: EPA Clean Ports grantee ports (53 grants, 26 states)
US surface mines trialing BEV haulage (Cat 793 XE ecosystem)
painful_job: Heavy fleets are electrifying ahead of charging infrastructure: mining majors build chargers in-house (Fortescue 6MW), three incompatible architectures compete, and no US vendor sells a ruggedized, standards-aligned multi-MW charging system for dust/vibration/harsh-duty sites.
product: A 1.5-6MW modular charging power system: SiC conversion stacks, liquid-cooled MCS dispensing per IEC TS 63379, sequencing/queueing control integrated with fleet-management APIs, containerized for port/rail/mine environments with storage-buffered grid connection options. Merged extensions: optional dual-standard capability - IEC MCS and Chinese swap-station DC interface personalities - plus a station-level DC-backbone architecture for multi-charger sites (from C-15); if P3R2-B-14's offshore-export gate fails, its dual-personality converter folds in here as the interface module (pre-agreed path).
frontier_vision: The refueling layer of heavy industry: ports, railroads, mines, then vessel shore/charge hybrids - power electronics as diesel's replacement logistics.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: duty-cycle and interconnection studies with one port and one railroad (public data + interviews); MCS standard tracking; power-stack architecture design. 2028: 350kW-1MW prototype stack validated at a university/utility test yard; thermal design of dispenser/cable per published megawatt-cable research. 2029: funded pilot commitment with an EPA Clean Ports grantee port.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: EPA Clean Ports equipment (already obligated, ~$3B) deploys 2026-2030, and FLXdrive/Cat BEV fleets move from pilot to scale in the early 2030s - the charging bottleneck lands exactly at launch while truck-focused EVSE vendors ignore harsh-duty sites.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Clean Ports-funded zero-emission equipment deliveries through 2030 (L10-048), Class-I railroad battery-locomotive scaling beyond initial FLXdrive orders (L10-032/051), and US mine BEV fleet decisions following the BHP/Rio/Cat Early Learner trials (L10-030); MCS certification wave post-IEC TS 63379 (L10-050).
competition_outlook_2030: ABB/Siemens/Kempower-class EVSE majors (truck-focused), Wabtec/Cat proprietary bundles (L10-051/053), Fortescue-style in-house builds (L10-029); the open, fleet-integrated harsh-duty niche remains contested but unowned.
timing_window_risk: US regulatory reversals (CARB repeal precedent, L10-044) can slow port/drayage demand; mining capex cycles; OEM-bundled charging could foreclose the merchant niche.
extreme_edge: 3,000A liquid-cooled charging in dust, vibration, and 45C ambient with autonomous-vehicle docking - grid power at diesel logistics tempo.
first_experiment: 2028: 1MW prototype charging stack + liquid-cooled dispenser executing a mining/rail duty cycle (repeated 10-minute megawatt sessions, 45C ambient chamber) with thermal and efficiency data published.
founder_fit_note: Controls, sequencing software, and embedded fleet-API integration transfer from the founder profile, but multi-megawatt conversion stacks, liquid-cooled dispensing hardware, and heavy-industrial site deployment are outside it.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed C-15 (dual-standard cabinet option, station-DC-backbone concept; its CN demand leg NOT imported - demand-without-access vs vertically integrated domestic stacks) and registered B-14's fold-in path as the interface-personality module. A-21 remains US-primary.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical heavy-fleet charging. Top-decile demand evidence (obligated EPA funds, named fleets); elegance mediocre (integration play); OEM-bundled charging and regulatory reversal are the risks. Import C-15 dual-standard option, B-14 interface-personality module.

concept: Modular plasma destruction units for concentrated PFAS waste streams (spent AFFF, IX regenerant, membrane reject) for DoD and water utilities
named_buyer_examples: US DoD installation restoration programs (PyroGenesis PFAS contract precedent)
municipal utilities with PFAS concentrate streams
painful_job: DoD has already paid for plasma PFAS destruction (>$2.25M, >300 tonnes treated) but the supplier base is one thin foreign small-cap with MW-torch heritage; utilities and bases generate concentrated PFAS side-streams with no scalable, verifiable on-site destruction option.
product: A containerized non-thermal/thermal-hybrid plasma destruction unit sized for concentrate streams (100-2,000 L/day), with nanosecond-pulsed power supply lineage, integrated fluoride capture, and destruction-efficiency verification instrumentation producing regulator-grade records.
frontier_vision: Distributed molecular incineration: PFAS first, then other halogenated and pharma waste streams - plasma chemistry productized around verified destruction, not tonnage.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university plasma-chemistry lab campaign on destruction efficiency vs energy per liter for AFFF/IX-brine surrogates; verification chemistry method development. 2028: skid-scale prototype (10-50 L/day) achieving >99.99% destruction on real concentrates under a DoD-lab or SERDP-style collaboration. 2029: demonstration at one base or utility; cost-per-liter dossier vs SCWO competitors.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: DoD AFFF transition and site investigations convert into remediation records-of-decision and destruction procurements in the early 2030s; the demonstrated-but-thin supplier base leaves room for a US modular entrant.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: DoD installation-restoration destruction procurements 2030-2034 following completed AFFF transition (paid precedent L01-036; torch-scale defense buying L01-034/035); utility concentrate-destruction demand as PFAS treatment residuals accumulate (inference - EPA drivers must be re-sourced in P4; flagged).
competition_outlook_2030: PyroGenesis (torch heritage), SCWO vendors (374Water/Aquagga-class), electrochemical oxidation startups, incineration incumbents under regulatory pressure; verified on-site modularity is the differentiator.
timing_window_risk: Destruction-technology selection could tip to SCWO if plasma energy economics disappoint; regulatory acceptance of destruction verification methods is unsettled.
extreme_edge: Breaking the strongest single bond in organic chemistry (C-F) at >99.99% with auditable evidence, in a shipping container.
first_experiment: 2027-2028: skid prototype demonstrating >99.99% PFOA/PFOS destruction on real AFFF concentrate at <=50 kWh/kg-PFAS with closed fluorine mass balance, verified by an independent analytical lab.
founder_fit_note: The nanosecond-pulsed power supply and destruction-verification instrumentation align with founder circuits, electromagnetics, and instrumentation skills, while plasma-process chemistry, fluoride handling, and regulatory validation are outside the profile.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Paid DoD precedent anchors an enthusiasm-zone lane correctly; verification-chemistry wedge; SCWO competition named; EPA drivers to re-source in P4.

concept: Sealed negative-pressure (subatmospheric) water/low-GWP two-phase cold-plate loop for >2 kW/chip Chinese AI racks - leak-safe by physics (air leaks in, coolant never leaks out), PFAS-free by design
named_buyer_examples: Envicool (Shenzhen CDU/thermal vendor, one of 7 vendors building Google Deschutes-class CDUs, L14-043)
Alibaba-class hyperscale operators already running HVDC datacenters (L02-048, L02-049)
Chinese colocation operators facing GB 40879 PUE caps (L14-035)
painful_job: Chinese operators must hit PUE <=1.25/1.2 caps (GB 40879 revision, L14-035) and 'high-efficiency cooling' policy direction (Guo-Neng-Fa-Ke-Ji [2026] No.34, L14-036 - NB: primary text has NO 100%-liquid-cooling mandate) while chip TDP heads to 3-5 kW and 800-1,000 W/cm2 (L14-010), beyond single-phase cold-plate margins; two-phase alternatives historically depended on discontinued PFAS dielectric fluids (two-phase literature L14-001/002/003/029).
product: A rack-level two-phase loop that runs water or low-GWP fluid below atmospheric pressure through microchannel evaporator cold plates (mechanism: saturation temperature tuned via vacuum setpoint; latent heat absorbs >2 kW/chip at near-isothermal junction; any seal failure leaks air inward, detected by vacuum decay, instead of coolant onto boards). Ships as evaporator plates + manifold + compact vacuum-condenser CDU compatible with T/CIEP 0263-2025 interfaces (L14-039).
frontier_vision: Become the default leak-safe two-phase thermal architecture for East-Asian AI infrastructure, then extend the same negative-pressure loop to power electronics, EV fast-charge cabinets and aerospace power-dense converters.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: university/lab-hosted loop testbed; replicate ITRI-class low-pressure results (L14-053 reports 2.4 kW/chip) on a 1 kW simulated die; file 2-3 core patents (vacuum-setpoint control, inward-leak detection, manifold blind-mate). 2027-2028: joint evaluation agreements (pre-company MOUs) with one Chinese thermal vendor and one TW/JP ODM lab; publish peer-reviewed validation. 2028-2029: 20-50 kW rack demonstrator with a partner lab in HK/Shenzhen; customer discovery with 10+ Chinese operators/ODM thermal teams on GB 40879 compliance economics; qualify supply chain for pumps/vacuum hardware.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Single-phase cold plates commoditize by 2030 in China, but >2 kW/chip parts push past single-phase margins exactly as hub-node PUE caps tighten; two-phase is still non-converged in 2026 (four competing architectures, L12-021-class CPO thermal debate; L14-001/002/003) so a 2030 entrant with a leak-safe niche is early, not late.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: (a) GB 40879 revised PUE caps (<=1.25 new large DCs, <=1.2 national hub nodes) enforced through 2030-2034 buildout (L14-035); (b) East-Data-West-Computing (Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan) hub-node datacenter parks explicitly referenced as active buildout in accepted CN sources (L02-049); (c) national AI-energy action plan cooling-efficiency line (L14-036).
competition_outlook_2030: Envicool and other Chinese CDU incumbents (7+ vendors already building to Deschutes spec, L14-043) will dominate single-phase; ITRI-fed Taiwan ODM two-phase lines (L14-053) are the closest technical rival; expect at least one Chinese two-phase startup. Differentiator must be the negative-pressure leak-safety mechanism plus fluid-supply independence.
timing_window_risk: Foreign-founder access: direct sales to Chinese hyperscalers unrealistic; route is IP licensing/joint development with a Chinese thermal vendor (Envicool-class) or ODM design-in via HK entity. Risk that Chinese incumbents productize two-phase in-house before 2030; mitigated by patent position and the documented non-convergence of two-phase architectures.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06-30: kill if no Chinese or Taiwanese ODM/thermal-vendor paid pilot (>=100 kW deployment) by mid-2034.
extreme_edge: Same loop physics scales to 1,000 W/cm2-class WBG power modules and directed-energy thermal buses.
first_experiment: 2027-Q2: side-by-side test of negative-pressure water two-phase plate vs best commercial single-phase plate on a 1-2 kW thermal test die; success = >=30% lower junction-to-coolant resistance at equal pump power plus demonstrated inward-leak fail-safe.
founder_fit_note: Vacuum-setpoint control, vacuum-decay leak-detection instrumentation, and the loop control unit fit founder controls and sensing skills, but the governing two-phase thermal-fluid and evaporator/manifold design work is outside the profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: no content imported. C-04 (FIX) adopts B-01's negative-pressure architecture as its primary variant for the US+CN dual play; B-01 remains the CN-primary canonical with the licensing/ODM access route. Cluster duplicate C-04 is retained as a separate FIX seed per adjudication.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Best China-primary seed and only mechanism-level original in thermal family (negative-pressure leak-safe two-phase, PFAS-free by design); correctly rejects the debunked 100%-liquid-mandate claim; access via licensing/ODM honestly planned.

concept: Electrostatic-chuck power/control subsystem: HV clamp supply with Johnsen-Rahbek leakage compensation, fast declamp, and multi-zone temperature-uniformity electronics for Chinese tool OEMs
named_buyer_examples: Chinese ESC/ceramics houses needing the electronics half (component-localization gap context, L06-054)
painful_job: ESC behavior is an unsolved coupled problem - dielectric breakdown limits (L06-005), Johnsen-Rahbek charging/discharge dynamics under plasma (L06-006), AlN resistivity tuning (L06-014), and thermal-uniformity control (L06-007, L06-015) - and a Chinese design-space framework paper (L06-004) shows domestic developers are attacking it now; but Chinese OEMs still import the qualified clamp-power/control stack, part of the <12% RF/precision-component localization gap (L06-054).
product: A qualified ESC electronics module: +/-kV programmable clamp supply with real-time leakage-current-based clamp-force estimation (mechanism: JR contact-current model inversion), arc-safe fast declamp with residual-charge neutralization sequencing, and 10+ zone heater drive with mK-class uniformity control - sold to OEMs and Chinese ceramics makers as the electronic half of a domestic ESC stack. Initial scope (FIX): mature-node (>=28nm) and panel/display tools only, for export-control safety; leading-edge sockets deferred until counsel clears.
frontier_vision: Standard electronics layer for every electrostatic clamping application - semicon, panel, e-beam tools, space robotics grippers.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: lab testbed replicating JR clamp physics on commercial ceramic pucks; publish clamp-force estimation validation; patents on leakage-based force observer + declamp sequencing. 2027-2028: co-development with a university plasma lab to characterize declamp-induced wafer displacement; build reliability data. 2028-2029: joint-evaluation with one Chinese ceramics maker + one OEM dev chamber; pre-qualify supply chain.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Chinese ESC localization is behind whole-tool localization; ceramics capability is emerging domestically (L06-004 research base) but the control-electronics half has no focused domestic supplier - 2030 lands after ceramics mature and before electronics commoditize.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: same 15th-FYP OEM supplier-localization wave (Hengyunchang/Injet precedent L06-042/044; component gap L06-054) extended to ESC subsystems as AMEC/NAURA move to higher-aspect-ratio etch where clamp/thermal control binds (etch growth documented L06-048). FIX condition: this trigger is inferential (localization gap + research base), not a named ESC tender; P4 must produce a named ESC-localization program/tender or a ceramics-maker co-development LOI before this seed can be promoted.
competition_outlook_2030: Incumbent ESC vendors (US/JP) ship integrated solutions; Chinese ceramics entrants will seek electronics partners rather than build - the seed's channel; risk of AE/MKS bundling clamp supplies (L06-039). P4 verification task: confirm AE/MKS clamp-supply bundling (L06-039) does not foreclose the merchant socket.
timing_window_risk: Same export-control caution as B-05 (advanced-node exposure) though ESC electronics are less node-specific; foreign founder should target mature-node and panel/display tools first. Flag: ESC-specific China demand evidence is inferential (L06-004 + localization gap), not a named tender - verify in P4.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if no ceramics-maker or OEM co-development agreement by end-2033.
extreme_edge: Clamp-force observer physics reused for electrostatic handling in vacuum robotics and lithography reticle chucks.
first_experiment: 2027-Q3: demonstrate real-time clamp-force estimation within +/-10% of load-cell ground truth across temperature and plasma-exposure aging on a JR ceramic puck; success includes <100 ms residual-charge-safe declamp.
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: the HV programmable clamp supply, leakage-current-based force estimation, declamp sequencing, and multi-zone mK-class thermal control are direct applications of founder circuits, controls, instrumentation, and embedded-systems skills.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: product scoped to mature-node/panel tools; demand explicitly labeled inferential pending a named ESC-localization program/tender or ceramics-maker LOI (P4 gate); AE/MKS bundling-foreclosure check added.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Genuinely under-picked subsystem; demand currently inferential (self-flagged).

concept: Dual-standard megawatt charging/battery-swap interface power conversion: one qualified converter family bridging Chinese GB/T swap ecosystems and IEC TS 63379 MCS static charging for exported mining/port fleets
named_buyer_examples: XCMG - 240t BEV truck supplier to Fortescue with phased 2028-2030 deliveries into an MCS/static-charging market (L10-028)
SPIC Qiyuan - battery-swap mining-truck technology exporter (Oyu Tolgoi deployment, L10-031)
Chinese swap-standard ecosystem players under the national excavator battery-swap standard (L10-046) and CCS battery-ship rules (L10-045)
painful_job: China bet on battery-swap across trucks, excavators and vessels (L10-031, L10-040, L10-045, L10-046) while Western mine/port buyers standardize on CharIN/IEC MCS static megawatt charging (IEC TS 63379, published Feb-2026, L10-050); the atlas documents no cross-recognition between the two ecosystems, so Chinese OEMs exporting equipment (XCMG-Fortescue, L10-028; Liebherr trolley-compatible trucks, L10-052) must engineer bespoke power interfaces per project - a recurring, standards-driven integration tax.
product: A qualified bidirectional 1-3.75 MW DC converter family with swappable interface personalities: GB/T swap-station DC bus profile on one side, IEC TS 63379 MCS (up to 1,250 V/3,000 A) on the other (mechanism: modular SiC DC-DC bricks + certified protocol/interlock stacks for each standard), enabling one truck/vessel platform to serve both ecosystems.
frontier_vision: The interoperability layer of global heavy-fleet electrification - every exported Chinese machine and every mixed-standard mine site runs through our conversion/interlock stack.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: standards deep-dive + protocol/interlock reference implementation for IEC TS 63379 and GB swap profiles; desk validation with CharIN documentation; patents on dual-personality safety interlock arbitration. 2027-2028: 250 kW brick prototype demonstrating both profiles on a battery emulator; publish interop test methodology. 2028-2029: field data partnership targeting one OEM export program (XCMG/SPIC channel via HK entity); pre-certification plan with a test lab. FIX gate (2028): documented evidence that one named export program (XCMG-Fortescue delivery chain or an SPIC swap-export follow-on) will procure dual-standard interface hardware; without it the standalone thesis ends.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: XCMG's Fortescue deliveries run 2028-2030 (L10-028) and swap-vs-MCS non-convergence is documented as of 2026; by 2030 the dual-ecosystem friction is an operating cost with named fleets on both sides - and no converged standard is plausible given three architectures still competing (L10-028/029/031 pattern).
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: (a) XCMG-Fortescue phased 240t BEV deliveries 2028-2030 and follow-on Western-market export bids (L10-028); (b) SPIC swap-technology export expansion after Oyu Tolgoi trial concludes end-2026 (L10-031); (c) domestic SOE open-pit equipment tender cadence (China Gold 60-truck-class tenders, L10-034; Baogang retrofit programs, L10-035) electrifying under swap standards (L10-046).
competition_outlook_2030: OEM in-house power teams (XCMG), CATL swap ecosystem (L10-031/040), Western charger vendors implementing MCS only. The neutral dual-standard niche survives if certification burden stays high - which IEC TS 63379's 2026 vintage suggests.
timing_window_risk: Foreign founder is actually advantaged on the IEC/Western-certification side; China-side access via OEM design-in. Risk: a swap-standard export failure (Western mines rejecting swap) would collapse the dual-standard need into MCS-only - monitor Fortescue/Rio architecture choices (L10-028/031). Fold-in path (pre-agreed per adjudication): if the swap architecture fails offshore, the dual-personality converter folds into P3R2-A-21 as its interface-personality module rather than continuing standalone. Demand classification: contingent (an option on standards non-convergence), not a base-demand seed.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06-30: kill if no OEM co-development agreement by mid-2034 or if MCS-vs-swap converges to one survivor before 2032.
extreme_edge: Protocol-arbitrated MW interfaces extend to port cranes, e-vessel shore/swap hybrid stations (L10-040/045).
first_experiment: 2028-Q1: 250 kW dual-personality converter passes scripted conformance suites for both IEC TS 63379 signaling and a GB swap-station DC profile on a battery emulator, including cross-standard fault-interlock scenarios.
founder_fit_note: Modular converter control, protocol/interlock firmware, and qualification test design draw on founder circuits, embedded, and controls skills, but megawatt-class power hardware engineering and dual-market certification logistics are outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 named-export-program procurement-evidence gate added; fold-in path to A-21 pre-agreed; demand reclassified as contingent standards-arbitrage option, not base demand.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Genuine standards-arbitrage insight; contingent demand, not a base.

concept: DC-link capacitor and converter health prognostics module: embedded ripple/ESR/thermal sensing with physics-based remaining-useful-life models for Chinese HVDC datacenter and industrial converter fleets
named_buyer_examples: Chinese rack/UPS/HVDC power vendors (Delta China L02-048, Autrans L02-049)
State-Grid-supply-chain converter makers as expansion (XJ Electric tender scale, L08-034)
painful_job: DC-link capacitors are a leading converter failure mechanism and higher power density compresses their thermal margin (canonical reliability physics, L02-010); China's 800V datacenter wave multiplies capacitor-dense converters in mission-critical service (L02-054, L02-048) with no standard embedded prognostics layer - fleet operators discover failures reactively.
product: A retrofit/embedded sensing module: in-situ ESR/capacitance tracking via existing ripple excitation (mechanism: synchronous demodulation of switching ripple - no injected signals), hotspot thermal estimation, and physics-of-failure RUL models (electrolyte loss / film self-healing statistics), reporting into vendor fleet software. Hardware+model product, not analytics consulting. FIX: defined as a retrofit SKU (clip-on sensing module + gateway) with the cross-vendor failure database as the compounding moat - not vendor-bespoke analytics.
frontier_vision: The reliability nervous system for the world's converter fleets - datacenters, HVDC valves, EV chargers, drives.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: accelerated capacitor-aging lab; validate ripple-demodulation ESR tracking against LCR ground truth across aging; publish. 2027-2028: module prototype in a live 30 kW converter; RUL model calibration; patents on excitation-free tracking. 2028-2029: fleet pilot with one Chinese PSU vendor (design-in via HK); failure-prediction scorecard. FIX gate (2028): LOI from one named design-win path - a PSU/UPS vendor or a third-party maintenance operator; absent it, treat as a feature (sell/license the IP) rather than a company.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Prognostics value scales with installed fleet age; China's HVDC/800V fleet installed 2026-2029 hits mid-life exactly at 2030-2034, when unplanned failures become boardroom issues; embedded prognostics resists commoditization via failure-database network effects.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: maintenance cycles of the Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan-era HVDC datacenter fleet (L02-048/049) and 800V rollouts (L02-054, L02-043) reaching capacitor mid-life 2030-2034; NVIDIA-spec maintenance-cost framing (up to 70% reduction claims, L02-043) shows operators already buy on maintenance economics.
competition_outlook_2030: Converter vendors' in-house telemetry (default), test-equipment majors, academic spinoffs. Win via excitation-free retrofit capability and cross-vendor failure statistics.
timing_window_risk: Risk of being a feature, not a product - mitigate by also serving third-party maintenance operators; access via vendor design-in; moderate. Keep the US secondary market active from day one so the business is not hostage to CN vendor access.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if no vendor design-win by end-2033.
extreme_edge: Same sensing generalizes to IGBT/SiC die-attach degradation tracking.
first_experiment: 2027-Q1: excitation-free ESR tracking stays within 5% of LCR-meter ground truth across a 2,000 h accelerated-aging run on 20 capacitors inside an operating 10 kW converter, correctly rank-ordering end-of-life.
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: synchronous-demodulation ripple sensing, hotspot thermal estimation, and physics-of-failure remaining-useful-life modeling map directly onto founder instrumentation, circuits/signal-processing, embedded, and numerical-analysis skills.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: retrofit SKU + cross-vendor failure-database moat made explicit; 2028 named design-win LOI gate added; US secondary market kept active to avoid CN-access dependence.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Elegant excitation-free ESR/RUL sensing; feature-not-product is the honest risk.

concept: 800-VDC rack power-path protection: integrated solid-state breaker + precharge/inrush control + insulation monitoring + hot-swap sequencing unit for AI racks and power sidecars
named_buyer_examples: Alibaba datacenter power suppliers e.g. Shenzhen Autrans (CN)
painful_job: US: 800VDC racks specified by NVIDIA/OCP have no converged DC fault-interruption, precharge, grounding, or hot-swap solution (L02-043, L02-044; SSCB still research-stage L08-017, L08-001/003/004). CN: Huawei-ecosystem trade engineering flags 800V DC protection, grounding, hot-plug, and inlet-node oscillation as unresolved integration pain (L02-054); China HVDC datacenter penetration was only ~12% vs 78% UPS, so the conversion wave is ahead, not behind (L02-048).
product: A shelf-format 800V/250-500A hybrid solid-state protection-and-sequencing unit: <100 us fault clearing, arc-signature detection (L08-018/019/020/021 methods), managed precharge, IMD, and blind-mate hot-swap logic, pre-certified to IEC 62477-1 (L02-112). Imports from merged duplicates: fleet-wide DC arc-signature analytics with a UL/IEC listing-path emphasis (from A-01); synthesized-virtual-impedance active bus damping and capacitor-health (ripple/ESR) telemetry (from B-03); arc rate-of-change dataset moat built from accumulated fault-signature libraries (from E-01).
frontier_vision: Become the de facto 'circuit-breaker layer' for all DC-native infrastructure: 800V racks, 1500V sidecars, MVDC campus feeders, EV megawatt charging, and DC industrial parks.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: publish comparative SSCB topology benchmark from lab work; join OCP power workstream; file 2-3 provisional patents on combined precharge+breaking+arc-discrimination. 2027: decisive brassboard experiment (below). 2028: second-gen unit tested in a partner rack lab (integrator MOU, no company needed); UL/IEC pre-compliance data. 2029: design-in discussions with 2 US integrators and 1 CN ODM via licensing LOIs; refresh 2029 market/pricing evidence before incorporation.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: 800VDC ramps 2027-2029 with ad hoc protection; by 2030 hyperscalers demand standardized, certified protection at gigawatt-campus scale while DC breakers remain unconverged (L08-001/003/004/017) - a component vacuum a focused entrant can own.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: post-2027 NVIDIA Kyber/Rubin-Ultra 800VDC production architecture scaling into 2030-2034 gigawatt campus buildouts (L02-043) plus OCP Mount Diablo spec revisions institutionalizing DC power shelves (L02-044). CN: implementation cycle of the April 2026 national AI-energy action plan (Guo-Neng-Fa-Ke-Ji [2026] No.34, L14-036) and conversion of the historically low (~12%) HVDC penetration base in Chinese datacenters (L02-048).
competition_outlook_2030: US 2030: Eaton/Vertiv/Schneider integrated shelves, ABB/Siemens industrial DC breakers, 1-2 funded SSCB startups. CN 2030: Huawei in-house, Delta China, Autrans (L02-049), Innoscience-device-based designs (L02-050). Window: incumbents sell whole shelves; a certified merchant protection layer that all shelf vendors can embed is unoccupied.
timing_window_risk: Export-control separability: good - protection electronics are conventional industrial power electronics (largely EAR99); China variant can use domestic GaN/SiC (Innoscience, L02-050) built by a licensed CN entity while the US entity serves OCP customers; main risks are hyperscaler vertical integration, UL/IEC certification timeline, and any future US controls on high-performance power semiconductors. 2026 specs/prices must be refreshed in 2029.
extreme_edge: Sub-100-microsecond bidirectional fault interruption at 800V/500A in a hot-swappable shelf form factor, with arc discrimination that avoids nuisance trips at 1MW-rack transient levels.
first_experiment: Q2 2027: 800V/250A hybrid SSCB + precharge/hot-swap brassboard; demonstrate <100 us clearing into inductive fault, 10,000 hot-swap cycles, arc-signature discrimination on emulated rack bus; IEC 62477-1 pre-compliance test plan.
secondary_markets: TW: ODM co-design and manufacturing wedge - Delta and the Taipei 800VDC ecosystem build the racks this unit slots into (L02-037, L02-046) [from E-01]
JP: SiC device supply and co-qualification with ROHM-class 750V/1200V parts (L02-051) [from E-01]
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: fast fault-clearing circuits, arc-signature detection, precharge/IMD/hot-swap sequencing, and fleet telemetry sit within founder circuits, embedded-systems, controls, and data-acquisition skills at a rack-scale power class amenable to founder prototyping.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-01 (fleet arc-signature analytics, UL/IEC listing path), B-03 (virtual-impedance damping, capacitor ESR/ripple telemetry), B-04 (CN protection-stack variant; unique damping/telemetry content already covered), E-01 (arc-ROC dataset moat; TW Delta/COMPUTEX and JP ROHM supply wedges - best secondary-market logic in the cluster). China leg unchanged: already independently evidenced (L02-048/050/054, L14-036). Standing kill trigger: spec absorption by NVIDIA/OCP.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical 800V protection. Genuine independent US/CN demand legs; absorb A-01 fleet analytics, B-03 damping, E-01 ROC dataset + TW/JP wedges. Spec-absorption by NVIDIA/OCP is the standing kill trigger.

concept: Rack-level pulsed-load energy buffer: electrolytic-free 800V DC-link module (film caps + GaN partial-power converter) that damps GPU load transients and bus oscillation
painful_job: US: multi-stage conversion and DC-link electrolytic capacitors are leading converter failure mechanisms exactly as rack power density rises (L02-010, L02-043). CN: Chinese trade engineering explicitly names inlet-node voltage oscillation and hot-plug transients at 800V as unresolved (L02-054); AI-rack pulsed loads amplify both.
product: A 30-100 kW shelf module: metallized-film energy bank + GaN partial-power bidirectional converter providing 10:1 transient smoothing, active bus damping, and ride-through, replacing distributed electrolytic banks; telemetry for capacitor prognostics.
frontier_vision: Standard 'power shock absorber' for every pulsed-load electrical system: AI racks, EV megawatt chargers, pulsed industrial tools, and grid-edge nodes - the missing buffer layer between stochastic loads and clean sources.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: characterize GPU-rack pulse spectra from published OCP/NVIDIA material; simulate buffer sizing; provisional patents on partial-power buffer control. 2027: decisive demo (below). 2028: 100kW prototype in partner lab; reliability model vs electrolytic baseline (L02-010). 2029: integrator design-in LOIs; refresh competitive landscape (BBU vendors, Vicor-class modules). FIX additions: 2026-27 quantified cost-parity model vs distributed electrolytic banks and vs BBU shelves (the buffer must win on $/kW-cycle and reliability, not novelty); hard 2028 gate: one integrator evaluation agreement.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: 2027-2029 800V deployments will surface oscillation/reliability failures at fleet scale; by 2030 buyers will retrofit and spec-in buffering, before any standard component category exists.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: OCP power-shelf spec iterations for 600kW-1MW racks (L02-043/044) forcing a defined ride-through/buffer function 2030-2032. CN: AI-energy action plan efficiency push (L14-036) plus the Chinese HVDC conversion wave (L02-048) creating a parallel retrofit cycle.
competition_outlook_2030: US: Vicor-class dense modules (L02-113), BBU vendors (SiC BBUs already shipping, L02-051), PSU incumbents adding capacitance the crude way. CN: Huawei in-house, Delta China (L02-037). Facility-scale rotating buffers (Piller, L16-052) solve a different layer and validate the pain.
timing_window_risk: Commoditization risk is the main threat: if NVIDIA specs an in-rack buffer and Delta/Vicor ship it by 2029, the window narrows - kill trigger. Export separability good (industrial module, dual-entity manufacturable with local devices). Refresh 2026 rack-power assumptions (TDP/flow figures are inconsistent even now, L14-044) before committing capital. Codified kill trigger (FIX): if OCP/NVIDIA specs an in-rack buffer function or Delta/Vicor-class vendors ship an equivalent product by end-2029, fold this seed into P3R2-C-01 as its buffer/ride-through option and stop standalone investment.
extreme_edge: Handling megawatt-per-millisecond load slew at 800V with film-capacitor energy density via partial-power conversion, at electrolytic-bank cost parity.
first_experiment: 2027: 30kW brassboard buffer; reproduce published 800V inlet-oscillation profile and demonstrate 10:1 transient smoothing + active damping; 1,000h accelerated film-cap vs electrolytic reliability data.
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: the partial-power bidirectional converter, active bus-damping control, and capacitor-prognostics telemetry are circuits, controls, and embedded work at a prototypable rack scale within the founder profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: end-2029 fold-into-C-01 kill trigger codified; electrolytic-bank/BBU cost-parity quantification and 2028 integrator-eval gate added.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Clean mechanism; lives one spec revision from absorption.

concept: Medium-voltage solid-state transformer power block (2.4 MW class) converting 10-35 kV directly to 800 VDC for AI campuses, engineered against the IEC 60076 standards gap
primary_customer_archetype: datacenter developers, electrical EPCs, power-shelf OEMs
named_buyer_examples: OCP hyperscalers standardizing SST power units (US)
datacenter EPCs buying GE Vernova-class electrification packages (US)
Chinese hyperscaler campuses under the AI-energy action plan (CN)
painful_job: US: OCP Mount Diablo explicitly standardizes SST-based power units next to transformer-rectifier units (L02-044), but a decade of ARPA-E-funded MV SST work has not produced a certified deployable block (L02-033, L02-034, L02-036). CN: Chinese datacenters pioneered rack HVDC (L02-048) but lack an MV-to-800VDC stage; the 2026 AI-energy action plan pushes high-efficiency architectures (L14-036).
product: A modular 2.4 MW MVDC/MVAC-to-800VDC SST brick: SiC cell-stack, HF transformer with insulation coordination designed to close the documented IEC 60076 test mismatch (L02-036, L02-001), multiport-ready (L02-107), sold as a certified building block to shelf/skid OEMs rather than as a turnkey substation.
frontier_vision: Replace line-frequency transformer + rectifier chains everywhere power quality and footprint matter: datacenter campuses, EV megawatt hubs, industrial DC parks, and eventually distribution-grid nodes.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: Pre-company scope strictly limited (FIX): HF-insulation/partial-discharge dataset work and standards-body seats only - no converter-product development. 2026-28: university/consortium work (DC-GRIDS-adjacent, L02-034) on HF insulation + partial-discharge dataset targeting the standards gap; IEC/IEEE working-group participation so the 2029-2031 standard revision matches the design; publish; patents on insulation/cell-bypass. 2028 HARD GATE: demonstrable IEC 60076-24-class standard progress (committee draft or later) PLUS an OCP SST procurement signal (spec revision or hyperscaler RFI naming SST power units); if either is absent, re-batch this seed to the wildcard pool and do not raise or spend against the $25-60M v1 plan. 2028: 100kW cell-string experiment (below) only if the gate passes. 2029: LOI with one US hyperscaler pilot program and one CN integrator licensing conversation.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: The SST blocker is certification, not physics (L02-036); whoever holds a certification-ready design when OCP-era buyers scale 1MW racks in 2030-2033 wins a category incumbents have repeatedly failed to open despite 10+ years of funding (L02-033/034).
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: OCP Mount Diablo SST-power-unit track maturing into procurement specs 2030-2032 (L02-044) amid continuing multi-billion-dollar datacenter electrification orders (L08-033). CN: AI-energy action plan implementation plus green-power direct-connection framework (L14-036) favoring direct MV-DC coupling in new campuses.
competition_outlook_2030: US: Eaton/Schneider/Vertiv shelf incumbents, GE Vernova at grid scale (L02-115), Heron-class SST startups. CN: Huawei, Delta (L02-037), State-Grid-adjacent suppliers. Expect 2-3 credible SST entrants by 2030; certification depth and OCP alignment are the moats.
timing_window_risk: Highest-capex seed in this batch; standards timing may slip (the same lag that blocked incumbents). Export separability moderate: MV SiC stacks to CN may face controls; plan CN-entity build with domestic SiC. Kill if no certified MW-class field pilot by end-2033. All 2026 program facts (DC-GRIDS, OCP roadmap) must be re-verified in 2029. FIX: the 2028 standards+procurement gate is binding; a blocked certification category must not carry $25-60M capex.
extreme_edge: MV-to-LVDC conversion at >99% availability with HF isolation at 10-35kV - the hardest live insulation/thermal co-design problem in commercial power electronics.
first_experiment: 2028: 100kW MV cell-string with HF transformer; partial-discharge and thermal-cycling dataset against IEC 60076-24-relevant test regimes; publish certification-gap analysis with a standards body.
founder_fit_note: Converter cell control and telemetry transfer from founder circuits/controls skills, but medium-voltage insulation coordination, high-frequency transformer design, and MW-class certification work are outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: hard 2028 gate on IEC 60076-24-class standards progress plus an OCP SST procurement signal; pre-company scope restricted to HF-insulation/PD dataset and standards seats; re-batch-to-wildcard rule if the gate fails; confidence lowered medium->low (schedule-fragile category).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Certification-first thesis is a real insight against the atlas's MV-SST negative finding; schedule-fragile.
